game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **RGB Express:** Players manage a color-coded delivery service on a grid-based map, drawing distinct paths for multiple trucks (typically 2-4) by dragging a finger from each truck to its destination. Each truck must pick up its matching colored package and deliver it to the corresponding colored house, with the core loop involving path planning for all trucks before tapping "Play" to simulate their simultaneous movement. Progression spans 400 levels, gradually introducing new mechanics like bridges (allowing one-way or limited crossings), buttons (activating/deactivating barriers), and cargo swap zones, which increase the complexity of collision avoidance and delivery sequence. The objective is to successfully deliver all packages without any truck collisions, with failure requiring a redesign of the paths.
game_info.description: Manage a color-coded delivery service where trucks must pick up and deliver packages to matching colored houses. Plan paths for all trucks on a grid-based map, ensuring they don't collide during simultaneous movement. Complete increasingly complex levels featuring bridges, buttons, barriers, and cargo swap zones. Successfully deliver all packages without crashes to progress through the puzzle levels.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (←↑→↓): Navigate grid cursor to select cells and plan paths
Space: Confirm path node / Place waypoint for selected truck
Z: Select next truck for path planning
X: Clear current truck's path
R: Reset level and return to start screen
Enter: Start game from start screen / Execute planned routes (simulate truck movement)
Esc: Pause/Unpause game

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic path planning and single truck delivery. Verifies that a truck can pick up its package and deliver to the destination without obstacles. Tests core mechanics of path creation and execution.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Select the red truck, create a simple path to its red package, then continue path to red house. Execute the path and verify successful delivery. This validates the fundamental gameplay loop.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The red truck should follow the planned path, pick up the red package, and deliver it to the red house. Level completion should trigger with no collisions. Test is successful if the truck completes delivery and level advances.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing multi-truck coordination and collision avoidance. Verifies that multiple trucks can execute paths simultaneously without crashing into each other. Tests the core challenge of the game.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Plan non-intersecting paths for two trucks (red and blue). Create timing offsets by varying path lengths. Execute paths and verify both trucks complete deliveries without collision. This tests spatial planning and timing.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Both trucks should complete their deliveries successfully without colliding. The level should be marked as complete. Test is successful when all packages are delivered and no collision occurs between trucks.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing bridge mechanics and one-way passage constraints. Verifies that bridges allow passage in specified directions and prevent invalid crossings. Tests special tile mechanics.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Create a path that uses a bridge to cross over another truck's path. Verify the bridge allows one-way passage and the trucks don't collide when one uses the bridge. This validates bridge logic.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The truck using the bridge should pass over other paths without collision. Bridge direction constraints should be enforced. Test is successful if bridge mechanics work correctly and allow strategic path overlapping.

TEST_3.expected: The truck using the bridge should pass over other paths without collision. Bridge direction constraints should be enforced. Test is successful if bridge mechanics work correctly and allow strategic path overlapping.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing button and barrier mechanics. Verifies that pressing buttons activates/deactivates barriers correctly. Tests interactive level elements and sequencing.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Plan a path where a truck drives over a button to open a barrier for another truck. Verify the barrier state changes correctly and allows passage. This tests button-barrier interaction.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: When a truck crosses a button, the linked barrier should change state (open/close). Other trucks should be able to pass through opened barriers. Test is successful if button presses correctly control barriers.
TEST_4.description: Testing button and barrier mechanics. Verifies that pressing buttons activates/deactivates barriers correctly. Tests interactive level elements and sequencing.

TEST_4.expected: When a truck crosses a button, the linked barrier should change state (open/close). Other trucks should be able to pass through opened barriers. Test is successful if button presses correctly control barriers.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing cargo swap zone mechanics. Verifies that trucks can exchange packages in swap zones and deliver to different destinations. Tests advanced puzzle mechanics.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Route two trucks through a cargo swap zone where they exchange packages. Verify each truck then delivers to the other's original destination. This tests package transfer logic.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Both trucks should exchange packages in the swap zone and successfully deliver to their new destinations. Test is successful if package colors are correctly swapped and deliveries complete.
TEST_5.description: Testing cargo swap zone mechanics. Verifies that trucks can exchange packages in swap zones and deliver to different destinations. Tests advanced puzzle mechanics.

TEST_5.expected: Both trucks should exchange packages in the swap zone and successfully deliver to their new destinations. Test is successful if package colors are correctly swapped and deliveries complete.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing path validation and illegal move prevention. Verifies that invalid paths (out of bounds, through walls) are rejected. Tests game boundaries and constraints.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Attempt to create paths that go outside the grid or through obstacle tiles. Verify these paths are prevented or corrected. This ensures robust path validation.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Invalid path segments should be rejected or not created. The game should maintain valid paths only. Test is successful if all validation rules are enforced correctly.
TEST_6.description: Testing path validation and illegal move prevention. Verifies that invalid paths (out of bounds, through walls) are rejected. Tests game boundaries and constraints.

TEST_6.expected: Invalid path segments should be rejected or not created. The game should maintain valid paths only. Test is successful if all validation rules are enforced correctly.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing collision detection and failure state. Verifies that when trucks collide during execution, the game correctly detects it and triggers failure. Tests win/lose conditions.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: When trucks reach the same cell simultaneously, a collision should be detected. The game should enter GAME_OVER_LOSE state. Test is successful if collision detection triggers failure correctly.
TEST_7.description: Testing collision detection and failure state. Verifies that when trucks collide during execution, the game correctly detects it and triggers failure. Tests win/lose conditions.
TEST_7.expected: When trucks reach the same cell simultaneously, a collision should be detected. The game should enter GAME_OVER_LOSE state. Test is successful if collision detection triggers failure correctly.
